name: Hubab ibn al-Harith
title: Brave Salmani
shortDescription: A brave warrior from Kufa who, with love for the Ahl al-Bayt, bypassed Ibn Ziyad's guards and was martyred on Ashura.
fullStory:
Hubab ibn al-Harith al-Salmani was a valiant Shia and warrior from Kufa. Belonging to the Bani Salman clan, he always nurtured the desire to help the Prophet's (PBUH) family in his heart. When the siege of Kufa by Ibn Ziyad's forces intensified, Hubab realized he must reach Imam Hussain (AS) as soon as possible.

With great intelligence and bravery, he managed to escape through the checkpoints of Kufa and traveled secret routes to Karbala. His joining the Imam's caravan demonstrated an invincible will against oppression.

On the day of Ashura, Hubab ibn al-Harith appeared in the battlefield with a warrior's spirit. Highly skilled in swordsmanship, he fought manfully in the early hours of the war when the group attacks of the Kufans began.

In this fierce battle, shouting 'Allahu Akbar', he charged into the enemy ranks and, shoulder to shoulder with the Imam's loyal companions, created a lasting epic.

Finally, after much resistance and inflicting casualties on the enemy, he was targeted by a cowardly barrage of arrows and attained the great grace of martyrdom to sign his loyalty with his blood.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى حُبابِ بْنِ الحارِثِ السَّلْمانِيِّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Hubab ibn al-Harith al-Salmani.
